That distance had been already estimated to be something under 1,400 miles, so that the Arctic Pole of their recently fashioned world must be about 350 miles to the north, and the Antarctic about 350 miles to the south of the island. Easter Island, called by Verne the Isle of Paques, is the remote Pacific landfall that gives the novice captain Dick Sand his one reliable navigational fix during the *Pilgrim*'s storm-driven voyage. Sighting it, he reasons: "It can only be the Isle of Paques; there is no other in these parts."
